artifacts.content:
Mami,

I’m finally sitting down to answer your text from earlier. Today was long. After school I had practice until 5:30, then came home and tried to work on that APUSH outline, but my brain kept going back to all the heart stuff.

I keep thinking about March 2022, when I fainted during PE at Canyon Ridge and they sent me to Banner – University Medical Center Tucson. That’s when Dr. Priya Menon first said “mild hypertrophic cardiomyopathy” and started asking about Dad’s sudden cardiac arrest at 39 when he was working that double shift at the UPS depot on Silverlake Road. I still remember you and Dad whispering in the hallway about the $18,000 ICU bill and having to put it on that CareCredit plan. It makes me feel guilty even bringing up more tests.

At my last appointment she reminded me about the $1,200 genetic test and follow‑up echo she wants me to do, but I told her we’re trying to get through senior year first. I know we’re still paying off Dad’s hospital stuff, plus that $900 echocardiogram I had in June 2023 that insurance denied, and the $320 I owe Sonoran Behavioral Health because I missed those two appointments with Dr. Hart when everything got crazy with soccer.

I’m not trying to be difficult. I just don’t want to make money even more stressful. Maybe after graduation, if I’m staying in Tucson for U of A, it’ll be easier to schedule all of it.

Also, don’t forget: Isa’s choir concert is next Thursday at 7 at Canyon Ridge, and Miguel’s game is Saturday at 9 a.m. at the Pima fields. Coach Hernandez said I can help with the little kids after my game if I feel okay.

Love you,
Ethan

Personal note – health / soccer / money

Stuff I keep needing to remember but don’t want to say out loud all the time:

1. Heart history
- March 2022 fainting in PE at Canyon Ridge → Banner – University Medical Center Tucson → Dr. Menon said mild hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.
- Dad’s sudden cardiac arrest at 39 at the UPS depot on Silverlake Road = giant red flag. Family still on that $18,000 ICU CareCredit plan.
- Carmen got her pacemaker at Tucson Medical Center in 2019 after fainting at church; Isa passed out on her band trip in Feb 2023. Dr. Menon thinks maybe some inherited conduction thing and wants whole family screened.
- Insurance already denied my $900 echo from June 2023, so Mom’s scared more tests will jack up premiums.

2. Rhythm stuff
- July 2023 – wore that 14‑day Holter from Northwest Cardiology after near‑blackout running drills at Pima Youth Soccer Club. Showed supraventricular tachycardia.
- Dr. Menon thinks SVT is worse with Adderall XR 20 mg (started Oct 2021 from Dr. Kelsey Hart at Sonoran Behavioral Health). When they cut me off in May 2023 because of the $320 no‑show balance, palpitations actually calmed down.
- August 2024 sports physical at Canyon Ridge: abnormal EKG → atenolol bumped to 50 mg daily.

3. Dumb decisions
- April 2024 U of A house party: two Monster energy drinks + Luis’s vape + half a weed edible = St. Mary’s ER, HR >190. Menon said no more energy drinks or vaping, especially with this heart.

4. Big picture
- Need to balance senior season, college apps, and not wreck my heart just to impress scouts.

Hi Coach Hernandez,

Just wanted to give you a quick update and ask about winter camp scheduling.

After that near‑blackout running drills at Pima Youth Soccer Club last July, Dr. Menon at Northwest Cardiology had me wear a 14‑day Holter monitor. It showed runs of supraventricular tachycardia. She thinks the SVT might be made worse by the Adderall XR 20 mg I’ve been on since October 2021 from Dr. Kelsey Hart at Sonoran Behavioral Health. Funny thing is, when my ADHD meds got cut off briefly in May 2023 because I owed $320 in no‑show fees, the chest flutters actually calmed down for a bit.

Then in April this year I made a stupid choice at a house party near the University of Arizona. I mixed two Monster energy drinks, a couple hits off my friend Luis’s vape, and half a weed edible. My heart rate shot over 190 and I ended up at St. Mary’s ER. At my August 2024 sports physical at Canyon Ridge, my EKG was abnormal, so Dr. Menon increased my atenolol to 50 mg daily and told me absolutely no more energy drinks or vaping.

She still cleared me to play as long as I stay on meds, hydrate, and listen to my body. I haven’t had any more scary episodes at practice, and I’m still doing the Tuesday/Thursday assistant coaching blocks with the U‑10s without a problem.

If you’re okay with it, I’d like to stay on the winter camp coaching schedule, maybe just avoid being the one running the longest sprint stations in the afternoon heat. I can handle technical drills, passing patterns, and helping with the keepers.

Thanks for working with me on this and for not freaking my parents out more than they already are.

Ethan

Event: Cardiology follow‑up – Dr. Priya Menon
Date: Friday, 2024-10-25
Time: 3:30–4:15 p.m.
Location: Northwest Cardiology, Tucson (same office as Holter monitor last year)
Attendees: Me, Mom if she can get off work

Notes to self:
- Bring list of meds: atenolol 50 mg every morning; Adderall XR 20 mg (if still on it by then); any over‑the‑counter stuff.
- Ask again about timing for the $1,200 genetic test and repeat echo she recommended after the March 2022 PE fainting episode at Canyon Ridge that led to the mild hypertrophic cardiomyopathy diagnosis at Banner – University Medical Center Tucson.
- Mention family history: Dad’s sudden cardiac arrest at 39 at the UPS depot on Silverlake Road and the $18,000 ICU bill we’re still paying off; Abuela Carmen’s pacemaker at Tucson Medical Center in 2019 after fainting at church; Isa’s unexplained syncope on the Feb 2023 band trip.
- Insurance worries: remind her they denied my $900 echocardiogram from June 2023 and Mom’s scared more testing will raise premiums.
- Rhythm stuff: July 2023 14‑day Holter monitor showed SVT; palpitations were worse while on Adderall XR (started Oct 2021 with Dr. Hart at Sonoran Behavioral Health) and got better when meds were cut off in May 2023 over the $320 balance.
- Lifestyle: confess about the April 2024 U of A house party (two Monsters + Luis’s vape + half an edible → St. Mary’s ER, HR >190), and confirm I’ve been off energy drinks and vaping since she upped the atenolol in August 2024.
- Ask specifically about clearance for college soccer vs. just playing club/intramural at U of A.

Tía Ana,

I owe you a long update, so here it goes while I wait for my oatmeal to cool down.

Health stuff first so Abuela doesn’t worry: I’m still seeing Dr. Menon about the heart. Remember when I tested positive for COVID back on Nov 30, 2020 at the county drive‑through by the Tucson Convention Center and Abuela Carmen went to stay with you in Phoenix from Nov 30–Dec 21 so she wouldn’t catch it from me at 2841 W Camino Vista? After that, Dr. Alvarez at Sunrise Pediatrics made me follow that strict return‑to‑play plan—Dec 14–20 just 15–20 minutes of light jogging around the Canyon Ridge neighborhood, then Dec 21–27 adding non‑contact drills at the Pima Youth Soccer fields.

Since then, the bigger issue turned out to be my heart, not COVID. In March 2022 I fainted in PE at Canyon Ridge and ended up at Banner – University Medical Center Tucson, where Dr. Menon said I have mild hypertrophic cardiomyopathy and brought up how Dad had his sudden cardiac arrest at 39 at the UPS depot on Silverlake Road. She keeps mentioning a $1,200 genetic test and more echoes, but Mom is stressed because of the $18,000 ICU bill from Dad and the $900 echo insurance denied last June.

On top of that, she wants the whole family screened because Abuela had her pacemaker put in at Tucson Medical Center in 2019 after fainting at church, and Isa passed out on her band trip in Feb 2023. Mom’s worried more tests will raise the premiums again.

Besides all the medical drama, life is normal: I’m grinding through APUSH, still playing right wing for Canyon Ridge, and helping Coach Hernandez with the little kids at Pima on Tuesdays and Thursdays. Isa’s band is going to that festival in Phoenix again, so maybe I’ll see you there.

Love you,
Ethan

Journal – remembering 2020 COVID mess

Sometimes when a teammate gets sick, my brain jumps back to late November 2020.

We had that away game in Phoenix with my club team, and a few days later I found out I’d caught COVID‑19 from a teammate. Dad was furious at Coach Marco Ruiz for letting that kid play while his mom was literally waiting on his positive test result. I tested positive on Nov 30, 2020 at the county drive‑through site by the Tucson Convention Center.

That same day, Abuela Carmen packed a bag and went to stay with Tía Ana in Phoenix from Nov 30–Dec 21 because of her diabetes and high blood pressure. I stayed shut in my room at 2841 W Camino Vista from Nov 28 through Dec 12, only seeing Mom when she brought food to the door. She had to miss 10 days of work at the dental office on Grant Road because they considered her exposed through me.

On Dec 7, Dad used Mom’s Zoom account to set up a telehealth visit with Dr. Alvarez at Sunrise Pediatrics. She asked if I’d had any episodes of my heart racing like during those panic attacks in 9th grade finals at Canyon Ridge. I told her it was nothing like that—no chest pain, no shortness of breath, no fainting—just low‑grade fever, stuffy nose, and feeling wiped out.

By Dec 14 she cleared me to start that super specific return‑to‑play: Dec 14–20 only 15–20 minutes of light jogging around our neighborhood, then Dec 21–27 non‑contact drills at the Pima Youth Soccer fields. Meanwhile, Coach Hernandez took me off the Pima winter camp schedule from Dec 1–18 and wouldn’t let me back on the field until I brought a clearance note from my in‑person checkup on Dec 18.

Wild that all that felt huge then, and now the heart stuff is the bigger story.

Me: Yo, you still coming over for FIFA after practice?

Luis: Yeah, but I gotta be home by 10. My mom’s on my case.

Me: Same. Also I’m officially done with Monsters and your stupid vape lol.

Luis: 😂 still on that? You were fine last party.

Me: Bro, you forget April by U of A? Two Monster energy drinks + a couple hits off your vape + half that weed edible = St. Mary’s ER with my heart over 190. Then in August 2024 at the Canyon Ridge sports physical my EKG was jacked and Dr. Menon bumped my atenolol to 50 mg and said “no more energy drinks or vaping, seriously.”

Luis: True, that was scary. My bad.

Me: It’s not just that. Remember when I had to wear that 14‑day Holter monitor from Northwest Cardiology last July after I almost blacked out running drills at Pima? It showed supraventricular tachycardia. She thinks the SVT gets worse with the Adderall XR 20 mg Dr. Hart put me on back in Oct 2021. Low‑key, when Sonoran Behavioral cut me off in May 2023 cuz I owed $320 in no‑show fees, the palpitations actually chilled for a bit.

Luis: That’s messed up they cut your meds over money.

Me: Yeah. Plus all the family stuff—Dad’s cardiac arrest at 39 at that UPS depot on Silverlake Road, Abuela’s pacemaker at TMC, Isa passing out on that band trip in Feb 2023. Menon thinks it might be some inherited electrical issue and wants us all screened, but Mom’s tripping because insurance already denied my $900 echo last June.

Luis: Damn. Ok ok, water and Gatorade only tonight.

Me: Deal. Just bring chips. Mom’s making tacos.

Notes to self:

- Date Of Birth: 2007-09-14
- Fainted During After Exercise No: true
- Extreme Shortness Of Breath Yes: false
- Extreme Fatigue With Exercise Yes: false
- Chest Discomfort During Exercise Yes: false
- Heart Test Ordered No: true
- Unexplained Seizure Disorder Yes: false
- Exercise Induced Asthma Not Controlled Yes: false
- Covid Diagnosed No: false
- Still Having Covid Symptoms Yes: false
- Hospitalized For Covid Yes: false
- Mis C Diagnosis Yes: false
- Special Heart Lung Tests Yes: false
- Returned To Full Sports No: false
- Covid Exposure Past 3 Months Yes: false
- Covid Tested No: false
- Covid Vaccine Received No: false
